name: unmarshal_local_state_can_fail
code:
proof fn phi_1_unmarshal_local_state_can_fail(v: Value)
    requires
        VReplicaSetReconcileState::unmarshal(v) is Err,
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: unmarshal is uninterpreted with no totality axiom, so at_step_closure and unwrap_local_state_closure call .unwrap() on potentially-Err results — any predicate using these closures has undefined behavior on non-unmarshallable local states

name: at_step_closure_vacuous_on_bad_state
code:
proof fn phi_2_at_step_closure_vacuous_on_bad_state(v: Value, step: VReplicaSetRecStepView)
    requires
        VReplicaSetReconcileState::unmarshal(v) is Err,
    ensures
        at_step_closure(step)(v) == at_step_closure(VReplicaSetRecStepView::Init)(v),
{
}
reason: when unmarshal fails, unwrap() produces an arbitrary value, so at_step_closure cannot distinguish any two steps on corrupt local state — all reconcile step predicates collapse to the same truth value

name: has_pending_req_no_contains_key_guard
code:
proof fn phi_3_has_pending_req_no_contains_key_guard(controller_id: int, s: ClusterState, key: ObjectRef)
    requires
        !s.ongoing_reconciles(controller_id).contains_key(key),
    ensures
        !Cluster::at_expected_reconcile_states(controller_id, key, at_step_closure(VReplicaSetRecStepView::Init))(s),
{
}
reason: at_expected_reconcile_states guards on contains_key, but has_pending_req_msg does not — if these predicates are composed in contexts where the guard is missing, map indexing on absent keys yields arbitrary values

name: object_ref_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_5_object_ref_ignores_uid(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata.name is Some,
        v2.metadata.name is Some,
        v1.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v2.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v1.metadata.name == v2.metadata.name,
        v1.metadata.namespace == v2.metadata.namespace,
        v1.metadata.uid != v2.metadata.uid,
    ensures
        v1.object_ref() == v2.object_ref(),
{
}
reason: object_ref uses only kind/name/namespace and ignores uid, so a deleted-and-recreated VReplicaSet with a new uid maps to the same ObjectRef — the liveness lemma's ongoing_reconciles keyed by object_ref could conflate old and new instances
